watchlist.notes: Mega-cap tech, strong trend follower. 2026-05-28 pre-mkt: $310.93 RSI 79.0 EXTREME OVERBOUGHT, MACD hist +0.85, Stoch K 96.5, SMA 20 $293.38 ✓ (price 6% above). Multi-week extension — no chase. Wait for daily pullback toward SMA 20.

Cloud/AI leader. 2026-05-29 midday: $443.97 — MACD bullish cross FIRED (+1.28), RSI 67.4, above SMA 20 ($417.27). BUT: SMA 50 ($402.69) < SMA 200 ($458.43) — long-term swing-trend test FAILS. Half-trigger from prior week stale-cleared 5/22 EOD. Next catalyst: Microsoft Build 2026 (6/2–6/3). Watch for post-Build pullback that ALSO reclaims SMA 50 → SMA 200 (would need run to $460+) before a real swing setup is possible. Until then: PASS on long, no clean short either (SMA 20 reclaimed + MACD just crossed).

AI chip leader. 2026-05-29 EOD: ~$216 RSI 54.87, MACD hist -1.81 (still negative — gate failing), Stoch K 7.07 (extreme oversold), price barely above SMA 20 ($215.74). Post-earnings (5/20) pullback zone $215–$220 was reached but MACD-cross gate never fired; thesis fading. Only oversold-bounce setup remains if Stoch < 5 produces sharp reversal with MACD reverse. If MACD continues deteriorating into Week 5, drop from active watch.

High beta, momentum-driven. Daily SMA 50 < 200 still — day trade only until daily trend clarifies. 2026-05-14: ~$445 RSI ~70, above upper BB. Cooled somewhat but still extended. Day trade only on clean 5-min setups.

E-commerce + cloud. 2026-05-29 EOD: ~$272, RSI 60.05, MACD hist -1.01 (CLOSEST to zero in watch list — closer than AVGO and TSM), Stoch K 49.55, above SMA 20 ($268.74). MACD cross is the next gate event but no fresh catalyst on the calendar. Observe-only this week; if MACD crosses zero AND a catalyst surfaces (analyst day, AWS announcement, retail data), debate fresh. Re-evaluate 6/1 pre-market.

MACD hist still positive (+2.73), but Stoch K 90 OB and SMA 200 $666.53 overhead = neither long (overbought) nor short (MACD positive). Short thesis from prior weeks REVERSED 5/28. Search + cloud. **OWN POSITION** since 2026-05-20 ($387.07 × 51 sh, Communication Services). 2026-05-29 EOD: $381.62 unrealized -1.41% (-$277.95). Long-term SMA bull stack intact (SMA 50 $347.60 > SMA 200 $299.93). Manual watch trigger $375. -7% mechanical cut $359.98. SMA 50 break ($347.60, -8.9%) is the real thesis-break signal. See `memory/open_positions.md` for full management trail.

Chip sector. 2026-05-14: $445.40 RSI 76.1 — overbought. Long-term bullish. Observe-only until post-NVDA earnings (5/20 AMC) — NVDA print sets semiconductor sector direction for the week. Re-evaluate 5/21 pre-market.

S&P 500 ETF — PRIMARY market posture indicator. Pre-market routine classifies posture via SPY SMA relationships (see strategy.md Market Posture System): GREEN (above SMA 20 + SMA 20 > SMA 50) / CAUTION (below SMA 20 but above SMA 50) / RED (below SMA 50) / BEAR (below SMA 200). Old price-level trip-wire ($736) retired 2026-05-19 — it was stale. Also tradeable as day-trade vehicle on high-volume trend days.

Nasdaq 100 ETF — tech/growth sector posture proxy. Monitor QQQ SMA relationships alongside SPY: if QQQ is CAUTION while SPY is GREEN, tech-heavy setups face additional headwind. Also tradeable as day-trade vehicle. Old price-level trip-wire ($700) retired 2026-05-19.
SPDR Tech Sector ETF. Use as tech sector trend proxy AND tradeable swing setup. Strong trending instrument. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
SPDR Energy Sector ETF. Energy sector proxy AND tradeable swing setup. Excellent short candidate in risk-off environments. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

SPDR Healthcare Sector ETF. Defensive sector proxy AND tradeable. **Priority raised 2026-05-29 per Week 4 weekly review**: watchlist is tech-heavy after Week 3 AI-infrastructure additions; XLV provides rotational protection if XLK starts to cool (XLK closed Fri 5/29 with RSI 79+ and MACD positive — extreme). Run fresh indicator scan in 6/1 pre-market to set baseline. Good long candidate if XLK rolls and XLV holds SMA 20.
SPDR Financials Sector ETF. Rate-sensitive — short candidate when yield curve flattens. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
SPDR Industrials Sector ETF. Cyclical sector proxy. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
Eli Lilly — GLP-1/obesity drug leader, mega-cap biotech with strong multi-year momentum. High ATR, large moves. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
JPMorgan Chase — financial sector bellwether. Rate-sensitive, good both-direction plays around FOMC events. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
Goldman Sachs — investment bank, high beta to risk sentiment. Good short candidate in risk-off. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
Salesforce — enterprise SaaS, cloud leader. Earnings-driven moves, good swing setups around analyst days. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
Palo Alto Networks — cybersecurity leader. Strong institutional sponsorship, clean technical setups. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
Exxon Mobil — energy megacap. Oil price proxy, good short in energy downturns. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
Palantir — AI/defense analytics. High momentum, high ATR, good for both directions. Volatile — size accordingly. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

Broadcom — custom AI accelerators (Google TPU, Meta MTIA), networking silicon (switching/routing), VMware cloud infrastructure. Strong cash flow, dividend grower. High institutional ownership. **Week 4 lifecycle**: 5/28 PASS at 6/10 (tentative entry $414–$418), 5/28 midday+EOD half-trigger 2/3, 5/29 midday full-trigger 3/3 BLOCKED by chase rule (+3.1% intraday, tentative zone outrun by $22+). 5/29 EOD ~$440, 3/3 conditions held but no proposal. **6/1 pre-market priority #1**: if pullback to a fresh entry zone with confluence intact, re-debate Bull/Bear/Judge. If price keeps extending without a pullback, the move is gone — pass per ADR-0006. Next earnings ~Aug 2026.

TSMC — world's largest semiconductor foundry, manufactures chips for NVDA, AAPL, AMD, ARM. Critical AI supply chain chokepoint. NYSE ADR (not Taiwan exchange). Watch for geopolitical risk premium (Taiwan Strait). NVDA earnings 5/20 AMC will move TSM sympathetically. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

Arm Holdings — CPU architecture licensor. Every smartphone runs ARM; growing AI edge and data center deployment. High P/E, momentum-driven. Watch for licensing renewal catalysts (NVDA, AAPL, Qualcomm). High ATR relative to price — size accordingly. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

Constellation Energy — largest US nuclear fleet operator. Signed 20-year PPA with Microsoft to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 for AI data center power. Direct beneficiary of the AI energy buildout thesis. More stable than speculative SMR names — existing revenue, contracted cashflows. Watch for new hyperscaler PPA announcements as catalysts. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

Vistra — nuclear + natural gas power operator. Heavy Texas/ERCOT grid exposure — AI data centers in Texas are direct customers. Higher beta than CEG. Good both-direction play around energy policy and ERCOT pricing events. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

Nano Nuclear Energy — SPECULATIVE. Developing portable micro-nuclear reactors for remote/off-grid applications including AI edge computing. Very high ATR, low float, no revenue. Size at 50% of normal position max. Trade momentum and news catalysts, not fundamentals. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.

NuScale Power — SPECULATIVE. First SMR (small modular reactor) design to receive NRC design approval. Developing commercial SMR deployments. Very high ATR, binary event risk around regulatory milestones and partnership announcements. Size at 50% of normal position max. No indicator data yet — pre-market routine will initialize.
